text:
There was once a charming little town called Serenade. Its people loved birds; they had a sanctuary surrounded by lush greenery where exotic birds sang all day. Everyone knew the wise old owl who presided over the birds' meetings.

One autumn afternoon, something incredible happened at the sanctuary. The great owl, whose feathers were as silver as moonlight, called an assembly.

"In the quiet months, we birds celebrate the harvest moon," the owl announced. "But tonight, there's a new constellation forming high up in the sky."

Everyone flew over. They watched as a new moon shone amidst a magnificent shape—a Swan sailing through diamonds.

That moon became known as Luna Sereniata—the Swan of Serenade. Each evening, villagers would point at her and sing. "Oh Luna Sereniata, you're as graceful as we are!"

Then one night, while everyone was gathered around a bonfire, Luna fluttered close. She whispered secrets into ears that weren'*t ears at all
